mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 4 Series 435d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 4 Series 435d engine (N57S) typically costs £5,000–£8,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,100–£1,800. A timing chain replacement costs £1,500–£3,500. The N57S is a premium diesel requiring specialist expertise.

The N57S is generally robust but has known issues. Timing chain tensioner wear (pre-2015) is the most significant risk — the chain is rear-mounted, making replacement expensive. DPF and EGR issues are common with short-journey use. Regular maintenance and long journeys are essential. Post-2015 models have revised chain guides.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 4 Series 435d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner wear (cold-start rattle, £1,500–£3,500), DPF blockage (warning light, reduced power, £400–£1,200), EGR cooler failure (coolant loss, white smoke, £500–£1,200), and injector failure (rough idle, misfire, £300–£800 per injector). The twin-turbo system is generally robust but can suffer actuator wear.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 4 Series 435d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: 4 Series 435d values range from £10,000–£25,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £5,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine represents 20–80% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 4 Series 435d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 435d uses a single engine code: N57S. This is BMW's top-tier 3.0-litre twin-turbo diesel, producing 313bhp. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£5,000–£8,000). The job requires engine removal on the 4 Series, making it a major but worthwhile investment. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic piston-valve contact.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the F32/F33/F36 435d N57S generation — a single-generation engine. All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 4 Series 435d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent BMW specialist) or £120–£170/hr (London/SE dealer). The N57's inline-six layout is relatively accessible, but the rear-mounted timing chain adds complexity.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 4 Series 435d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Forced regeneration or cleaning — worth it on a well-maintained car
Replace all six together for reliability
Replacement often £5,000–£8,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 4 Series 435d? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing chain history (tensioner and guides replaced)
* Full service history with regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles)
avoidIf: * Pre-2015 with no timing chain history — the risk is real
* Any evidence of DPF or EGR issues — warning lights, reduced power
* Short-journey-only history — DPF issues are almost certain
* Poor service history — the N57 is unforgiving of extended oil changes

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 435d is a remarkable car — 313bhp, 630Nm and effortless refinement. The N57S is a powerful and characterful engine, but it demands respect for its timing chain and DPF/EGR systems. A post-2015 car with documented chain history and regular long journeys is the sweet spot. Buy on history, maintain the fuel and emissions systems, and the 435d will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 4 Series 435d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 4 Series 435d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Service History — 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes are essential; check for BMW LL-04 spec
* DPF History — Ask about driving patterns; frequent short trips cause DPF issues
* EGR System — Any coolant loss or white smoke? EGR cooler failure is common
* Injector Performance — Check for rough idle or hesitation; injectors wear on high-mileage cars
* Turbo Response — Test acceleration; any lag or whistling? (twin-turbo system)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? DPF and EGR codes are common
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
